title: lindernia dubia var. riparia
intro: Lindernia dubia var. riparia, also known as water-pimpernel, is a plant species that belongs to the Linderniaceae family, characterized by its delicate white flowers and creeping habit. Traditionally, Lindernia dubia var. riparia has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its ability to treat digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, contributing to its various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. In herbal medicine, Lindernia dubia var. riparia is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it can also be used topically as a poultice or salve to treat skin conditions and wounds.
